paper.title: A spectroscopic and kinematic survey of fast hot subdwarfs

extraction.summary: The paper explicitly concludes that none of the 53 fast hot subdwarf candidates is unbound to the Galaxy. All objects are judged bound: 48 are classified as extreme halo stars and 5 as thick-disc members. US 708 is mentioned only as a previously known prototype, not as a new candidate introduced or reassessed in this work.

method_chain.summary: Input from SDSS spectroscopic database, ground-based proper motion catalogues, and Gaia EDR3 astrometry.
Selection of fast sdO/B candidates with Galactic restframe velocities v_grf > 300 km/s.
Multi-epoch radial velocity measurements from SDSS, BOSS, LAMOST, and follow-up spectroscopy.
Removal of stars with inconsistent proper motions, composite/blended sources, and proto-He-WD misclassifications.
Homogeneous quantitative spectral analysis with ATLAS12/DETAIL/SURFACE to derive T_eff, log g, and helium abundance.
SED fitting with ATLAS12 synthetic spectra to determine angular diameter, reddening, and spectroscopic distances.
Spectroscopic distances derived from angular diameter and stellar radius assuming 0.47 M_sun canonical mass.
Calculation of Galactic velocity components U, V, W and total Galactic restframe velocity v_grf from RV, proper motions, and distances.
Adoption of Model I from Irrgang et al. (2013) for the Galactic gravitational potential.
Backward orbit integration to determine eccentricity, angular momentum L_Z, and last Galactic disc crossing parameters.
Assessment of bound versus unbound status based on Galactic restframe velocities and orbit integration. All 53 candidates are found to be bound to the Galaxy.
Evaluation of halo membership versus SN Ia ejection scenario based on kinematics, spectral diversity, and binary fraction.

candidate_groups_considered.description: Kinematic parameters of the 53 fast star candidates, including Galactic velocity components U, V, W and total Galactic restframe velocity v_grf.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: The paper explicitly states that all 53 fast hot subdwarf candidates are bound to the Galaxy. While some have Galactic restframe velocities close to the escape velocity, the paper concludes they are extreme halo stars rather than unbound HVS candidates.

source_refs.context: Abstract states none of the stars is unbound to the Galaxy.
Kinematic analysis section states all stars are bound to the Galaxy.
Conclusion states no unbound HVS sdO/B was found among the sample.

candidate_groups_considered.description: Parameters of the 53 fast star candidates at the last Galactic disc passage, including ejection velocity v_ej, time since crossing t_disc, eccentricity e, and angular momentum L_Z.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: The paper evaluates whether these stars could have been ejected from the Galactic disc but concludes that the full sample is more consistent with a pure old Galactic halo population. Only 21 of 53 stars crossed the disc within their sdO/B lifetimes and could theoretically be SN Ia ejection candidates, but the paper does not present any of them as likely unbound or strong ejected-companion candidates.

source_refs.context: Out of 53 stars, 36 could have been ejected from the disc within uncertainties, but 32% are very likely halo stars, making the SN ejection scenario unlikely for the full sample.
Conclusion explicitly states no strong candidates for bound ejected companions of SN Ia were found.
